round
my neck
an amu-
let
Be-
tween
my eyes
a star
A
ring
in my
nose
and a
gold
chain
to
Keep me
where

label: Romantic

text:
I lost morning runs around the living room
The TV blasting what I used to watch
I lost riding to school with my grandpa
Swimming with floaties, unable to touch
I lost my earliest years in Brussels
How autumn leaves wrapped me up
I lost the making of toy shops on the floor
And the way I cried when I had to clean them up
I lost stacking paints in a closet
The racket we’d make outside of class
I lost the newspaper I made at eight
It’s lost, just like the years that have passed
I lost hundreds of skipped lunches
I’ll be ****** but I miss them
I lost realising people weren’t my thing
And that I’m better off without them
I lost just now what helped me out
It dug me out of my grave
But you swooped in and pulled it away
After all I had and all I gave

So please don’t take this, it’s all I have left
Anything, anything but this
It’s the only thing I can cling onto anymore
Anything but this
label: Nostalgia

text:
It’s not a place you can visit.
It’s a fog that drifts through open windows
and sits beside you quietly,
smelling faintly of chalk dust and rain.
You think you’ve outgrown it
until it finds your heartbeat,
and suddenly you’re sixteen,
afraid of nothing
and missing everything.
label: Nostalgia

text:
Morning coffee, a familiar haze,
The quiet comfort of our shared gaze.
No grand pronouncements, no dramatic plea,
Just your hand resting gently on my knee.
In this small silence, everything is clear,
My favorite part of day, with you right here.
label: Romantic

text:
My name looked different
when you wrote it.
Rounder, slower, softer —
as if you knew
I’d grow into my edges too soon.
Now I sign it fast,
and it feels like forgery.
label: Nostalgia

text:
It suffocates, this blanket made of lead,
The weight of all the things I should have said.
It is so heavy, and it is so warm,
A self-inflicted and relentless storm.
I could just throw it off, and take a breath,
And cheat this slow and comfortable death.
But it is all I have, this heavy sheet,
This miserable and glorious defeat.
label: Misery

text:
They talk about death like they
Know it all
Those oblivious little creatures 
Just keep running around
They remind me of pawns in 
This ruthless game...
Unaware of the fact that the 
Loss is theirs anyways..

They talk about grief like
They have walked on that road
Just ask the ones 
Who grow with it ...
Like the forest of thorns..
It may seem greener , prettier and calming
But if you come any close
You will only bleed..

They talk about Loss
Like they have experienced it all
I laugh at how more pathetic can one be?
It's all the same ..
The first broken toy and the learnt lesson..
Sometimes the toys fade with time
And sometimes they take you with them..

They talk about it all..
Well , It's easy to do just that..
Just TALK..
I wish the words would realise
Just how much power they hold 
I wish they would just bend themselves 
Into healing flowers that 
Grow upon a dead soul
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
At lunchtime I bought a huge orange—
The size of it made us all laugh.
I peeled it and shared it with Robert and Dave—
They got quarters and I had a half.

And that orange, it made me so happy,
As ordinary things often do
Just lately. The shopping. A walk in the park.
This is peace and contentment. It’s new.

The rest of the day was quite easy.
I did all the jobs on my list
And enjoyed them and had some time over.
I love you. I’m glad I exist.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
So crowded...
So much so..
It leaves us completely empty..
The vessel that's the loudest..
But apparently...
Everyone becomes suddenly deaf..

So much similarities..
So much so..
I see my several reflections all around me
And yet....
Yet I fight the war completely alone..
A lone survivor..
It's quite sad..really...

So much yearning...
So much so..
I see the threads of longing hanging along..
Twisted and turned ..
Ending up as unresolvable knots..
Puppets with whims...
Just dancing dancing and dancing 
On the tones of their own dims..

Such eternity of misery
Sadness and heartbreaķs. .
Yearning and longing...
Emptiness and the dark glory..
It's all ...never-ending..
It's a lesson darling...
Learn...it's just...not worth it...
label: Misery

text:
I was numb 
Walking on a throny road 
Feet covered with blood 
Bearing a satisfying smile 
The hurt 
The pain 
The holes made by the thrones 
It was all gone 
Or it was there 
But left unnoticed? 
Unfelt? 
Suppressed beneath The heavy smile ....

I walked and walked and kept walking 
Until my feet bleed to The rivers of blood 
Then I fell 
I fell into a dark embrace full of light 
A light 
A cure 
I wasn't aware of ..
It was peaceful 
I was relieved Then 
Thought I have found my peace 

But then 
Something shook me to the core of 
My stomach 
My peace 
Was within me 
The light that I fell for 
Was no one but my own self ..
I was searching It in the outside world 
But forgot to search the world that was within me ...
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
The dripping tap, a form of water clock,
A steady and relentless, quiet shock
Against the silence of the sleeping house.
A conversation with a thirsty mouse,
Perhaps. A small insistence, sharp and clear,
That something is not perfect, something here
Is broken. Drip. A pause. And then again.
A tiny, silver hammer made of rain.
It measures out the night, it will not cease,
A slow erosion of the household's peace,
A testament to things left unresolved,
As the long, quiet hours are dissolved.
label: Everyday Life

text:
Call out across the canyon wide,
Your voice comes back to you.
The world reflects what's held inside—
Send joy, it will ring true.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
I find myslef weirdly courageous 
Giving in to manipulations of something 
greater than just emotions 
Yes babe 
I was tricked 
By not anyone else but my own self..
"Yeah , He looked at you "
"Maybe he does like me !"
"You know when we start dating....."
This shit sayings fucked my mind..
Intoxicated it ..
Yòu are just the infiltration..
A tresspasser ..
An intruder ..
Just a lovely chaos of crossed wires
Maybe I made you into something special..
More than just normal..
And then again you are actually that one of a kind..
I am so fucking proud 
Of my vexatious disclosure 
Regardless the outcome 
These are after all mere emotions
Don't have a sense of practicality 
They are blind , deaf and mute

But then again ..
That's why we are called "THE HUMANS" 
so lost and tangled 
Twisted and hurt 
Moving on from something that wasn't even real..
Just how pathetic that makes us? 
U see , 
That's the power of manipulations
label: Misery

text:
Across vast distances of space,
Light travels just to reach our eyes.
Each star's a messenger of grace—
Hope bridges all the skies.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
The neighbor’s cat, a shadow sleek and proud,
Upon a fence, above the madding crowd.
It judges all the world with half-closed eyes,
Indifferent to our hurried, small goodbyes.
A king enthroned on his suburban wall,
Who watches over, and ignores us all.
label: Everyday Life

text:
No doubt in Holland,
when van Gogh was a boy,
there were swans drifting
over the green sea
of the meadows, and no doubt
on some warm afternoon
he lay down and watched them,
and almost thought: this is everything.
What drove him
to get up and look further
is what saves this world,
even as it breaks
the hearts of men.
In the mines where he preached,
where he studied tenderness,
there were only men, all of them
streaked with dust.
For years he would reach
toward the darkness.
But no doubt, like all of us,
he finally remembered
everything, including the white birds
weightless and unaccountable,
floating around the towns
of grit and hopelessness––
and this is what would finish him:
not the gloom, which was only terrible,
but those last yellow fields, where clearly
nothing in the world mattered, or ever would,
but the insensible light.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
Last year 
Old friends 
Older laughs 
And oldest regrets
Crave for my attention 
For the last time 
Today ...
Say look back will you? 
The mistakes you made 
The thoughts you neglected 
The regrets your suppressed 
The laughs and smiles you swallowed 
The highs you chased 
The hurt that still stays 
Fresh as ever 
Still haunting you 
Like its here for it 
Forever 

New year 
New friends 
Newer laughs 
And newest regrets 
People walking in and out 
In the circle of life 
Tracing different trajectories every fucking time ..
Just ask this one question to yourself 
Will any of this make any difference?
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
The twigs remind me
of ancient a memory,
muted by time
and our efforts to forget.
You remember how
You’d snap a twig into two,
then we’d sword them off,
like two chivalrous knights,
queer knights that feared
a shared sunset together more than
Battle.
What happened next?
the next memory seems to
slip away from me too,
imitating you.
label: Nostalgia

text:
This being human is a guest house.
Every morning a new arrival.
A joy, a depression, a meanness,
some momentary awareness comes
as an unexpected visitor.
Welcome and entertain them all!
Even if they’re a crowd of sorrows,
who violently sweep your house
empty of its furniture,
still, treat each guest honorably.
He may be clearing you out
for some new delight.
The dark thought, the shame, the malice,
meet them at the door laughing,
and invite them in.
Be grateful for whoever comes,
because each has been sent
as a guide from beyond.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
I walk these streets ,
Filling me with thousands of memories 
Cutting right through 
Settling in the rusted corner 
That I abandoned ages ago ..

The talks 
The walks 
The dances 
And slight glances 
The intertwined hands 
Now alone here I stand 
Thinking about you 
Only you ...

Going through the phone 
Came across our clicks 
So young and beautiful 
So US
I wish I knew this back then 
You wouldnt feel so distant 
And far away now 
Out of my reach
Just maybe ..

But then again 
MAYBE's are hopless 
Aren't they?
label: Nostalgia

text:
Your love is not a harbor, safe and sound,
But a wild ocean where I gladly drowned.
It's not a shelter from the wind and rain,
But the thrilling, sweet, and terrifying hurricane.
I do not come to you to be at peace,
But for the beautiful, chaotic release.
label: Romantic

text:
A spider in the corner of the room,
Constructing architecture in the gloom.
She throws a thread, a hopeful, silken line,
A geometric, perfect, pure design.
It is a patient and a lonely art,
To build a world completely torn apart
By duster, or a hand, or just the breeze.
She does not care, she works to find her ease,
And builds again.
label: Everyday Life

text:
This was the first time I knelt 
and with my lips, frightened, kissed 
the lit inwardly pink petaled lips.

It was like touching a bird's exposed heart
with your tongue. 

Summer dawn flowing into the room parting the
curtains—the lamps dimming—breeze
rendered visible. Lightning,
 and then soft applause
from the leaves . . .

Almost children, we lay asleep in love listening to the 
rain.

We didn't ask to be born.
label: Romantic

text:
There was a time 
When I used to be just fine
Fine in such a way 
That made me shine 
I used to be a child then 
So innocent and raw 
Believed everything I heard 
And I saw
It was crystal clear 
Right in front of my eyes 
All the truths 
And all the lies.

Now this is the time
Where I struggle to be fine
Fine in such a way 
That would yield a smile..
I am eighteen now 
Have become an adult 
Surrounded by the blurs 
In all directions 
Nothing seems to work 
The way I want 
The harsh words 
And the taunts 
Should get used to them by now 
I don't know how I found this way 
I just don't know how ...

There will be a time 
When I would be fine 
Fine in such a way 
That I wouldn't whine 
I would be sixty then 
Wrinkles and whites 
But I bet I would be happy 
In the dimming lights 
Watching the sunset 
At a beach side 
Play with my grandkids 
And have a great ride 
I would escape the blurs 
Just fine 
I know 
It would turn out 
Just alright ...
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
It's a crisis 
All around and deep within 
Death and dark 
Everywhere you look 
Cruelty grows with time 
Almost exponentially 
Truths hide behind the dark cowards
They wait and claw out at the worst peaks
Lies decorate my world 
Make all of this seem beautiful 
I am fooled for a split second
But the colours turn to dust
The dwellers of the darkness 
Rise above and beyond 
They look very similar to the masks I see around ...
Or are they the same maybe?
They prey on themselves 
Watch them squirm and suffer
And they often get off on it 
These monsters then go back to sleep
Behind the ferocious facade of the exteriors..
The concepts of beauty make them easy to camouflage 
It's so wrecked and twisted 
It can't be fixed ..
Even if you and I want it to .
It's the world of death my friend
We just dance around until it strikes 
We are temporary somethings 
Waiting to fade into permanent nothings ..
So go on ..
Have it all .. 
It's just a facade after all
label: Misery

text:
The pulled-apart world scatters
its bad news like a brush fire,
the ink bleeds out the day's undoing
and here we are again: alive.

The tributary of this riverine dark
widens into the mind's brief break.
Let the flood come, the rowdy water
beasts are knocking now and now.

What's left of the woods is closing in.
Don't run. Open your mouth big
to the rising and hope to your god
your good heart knows how to swim.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
Once upon a time 
Born was a princess 
 To a kingdom named FIRE..
No one could tell if this was meant to be
It really felt like unerasable destiny 
The princess lived upto the name of her empire
She had this insanely strong desire 
To glow in the darkest darks
To burn like the FIRE 
To give birth to the warmth in winters 
And also burn down the sinners
She had it all 
As if chosen by the sun 
The burning beauty 
Greater than any man could ever be 
Strong and dangerous 
You couldn't get a closer look 
She was aware of it all 
Even the sound of the rushed breaths they took
But the glory has its end
The icebergs emerged out of nowhere 
Through the rush of ocean 
And pits of the deeps
Came the God Of ICE 
Her only wrath 
Who had the power to hold her down
And face her aftermaths..
The sworn enemies stood there 
In the open eye to eye ..
Glare to glare..
A battle of elements began ..
And hence the ruins
Who knows.. 
When where and how it would ever end?
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
The sailor sees the darkness fall,
The waves rise high like mountain peaks,
The wind tears at his ship and all
He loves—the wood cries out and shrieks.
But there, beyond the chaos wild,
A light revolves against the black,
A beacon shining, strong and mild,
A promise that he'll make it back.
The lighthouse keeper tends the flame,
Through every storm and every gale,
He doesn't know the sailor's name,
But guards the light lest hope should fail.
For generations, keepers came
To climb the spiral stairs each night,
To trim the wick and fuel the flame,
To hold back darkness with their light.
And though the world has changed its ways,
Though GPS now guides the ships,
That light still shines through foggy haze—
A symbol on a thousand lips.
It speaks of hope that never dies,
Of duty done with faithful care,
Of joy when morning clears the skies,
And those we love are waiting there.
So be a lighthouse where you stand,
Hold steady through the darkest night,
Reach out with your illumined hand,
And be somebody's guiding light.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
I am alive 
Still breathing 
Still walking 
Tirelessly..

Still loving 
Still hating
Wholeheartedly...

Still confused 
Still clear 
Ironically...

Still beautiful 
Still young 
So lively ...

In pain 
In grief 
Heartbreakingly..

Still helpless 
But hopeful 
Pathetically....

Dancing on the edge of life and death 
Trying not to fall 
Can't help but falling ..

Falling for something 
Far better than just "me" 
Falling for" us "
Honestly 
M so glad 
That I fell 
For 
"YOU!!!!!"
label: Romantic

text:
I read my favorite lines from my old books,
And find your meaning in the author's looks.
I hear a melody upon the air,
And trace the notes back to the way you care.
It seems that everything I love to do,
Has somehow found its way to lead to you.
label: Romantic

text:
The clink of spoon against a ceramic cup,
The dark brown coffee slowly filling up.
A simple ritual to start the day,
And chase the remnants of the dreams away.
This quiet moment, fragrant, warm, and deep,
While all the rest of the whole world's asleep.
label: Everyday Life

text:
That old streetlamp still flickers,
a heartbeat of the neighborhood.
Under its trembling light,
we traded secrets,
coins for ice creams,
and promises too small to last.
Now it burns alone,
guarding echoes of laughter
it can’t quite forget.
label: Nostalgia

text:
"The tear slipes down your cheek 
Trailing towards the chin yet again
U turn away from me 
And it hurts more than I can admit
The silent treatment drives me crazy 
Pushes me off edge , my insanity 
I wish i could make it all better 
Make it all hurt less 
Make it less of a mess 
Make it just STOP 
But I cant ..
Since I am the cause of the havoc 
That destroyed you 
Wrecked you 
Broke you 
Tore the shreds you were holding onto ...

You tremble and shiver 
The pain , the sin , the sinner 
That one look 
Is all it takes 
To make me breatheless 
Yes , i have no shame 
I have nothing to lose now 
I dont know how we ended up walking this road 
But we are here somehow 
I know i am not worthy of it 
But I still dare to ask 
Can you spare me 
This last kiss?
The last dance?
I will live with that 
Till eternity falls into the lonely embrace of nothingness. 
I will live with that 
Till there is no me left 
I will live with that 
Thinking of it as US 

Here it comes 
Torturing me to such an extent 
I struggle at every step 
That puts the distance between us 
The goodbye 
I didn't ever want 
Pokes me with a thousand needles 
Straight into my heart 
Its like i will never be alive again 
Not like i want to be 
I know its never easy 
But 
This is worse than difficult 
It is life threatening.."
label: Misery

text:
The quiet satisfaction of a peal,
An orange skin that keeps its form, so real.
A single, curling strip, a fragrant snake,
For nothing but the quiet process' sake.
The citrus stings the air, a sudden zest,
A small and aromatic, acid test.
The naked fruit, a perfect, glowing sphere,
That banishes the coldness of the year.
label: Everyday Life

text:
Is it too much? 
Have been thinkìng about it 
Everytime this ends with
 Admiring you 
For every petty thing that you do 
Cursing you 
Trillion thoughts fighting for hope
Maybe its already a lost battle
Maybe you are already mine 
Maybe its just a matter of time
Then it will be just you me and the sunshines..

Is it too much?? 
The agony you make me go through..
I am filled with violets and whites
And suddenly its the darkest of blues ..
What if 
I want all of them equally intertwined

And leave it to the world 
To find the difference...

Is it too much??
You making me dream 
The prettiest truth 
U and me and the evening lights 
Walks alongside the rivers 
These starry nights 
Its too good to be perfect 
You pinch my check 
Forcing me into the ugliest lie 
Where you are so close 
Yet miles away 
Where i just keep falling for you 
And you remain 
Aloof of this scene 
Leaving me in utter 
Dismay..
But oh darling 
What can i say?
I am shamelessly hopeful ;
In the darkest night 
You are my only moon
label: Romantic

text:
Anxiety’s a small and buzzing fly,
That circles endlessly around my eye.
There is no reason for it to be there,
A tiny monster in the heavy air.
I try to swat it, but it moves too fast,
A shadow that my own two hands have cast.
It is a small thing, but it never rests,
And builds a thousand miserable nests.
label: Misery

text:
I am a puzzle with a missing piece,
A flawed design that will not find release.
And people look and think they see the whole,
A functioning and self-sufficient soul.
They do not see the gap, the empty space,
The jagged, lonely, and unfinished place.
And I just smile, and let them think it's true,
The missing piece, of course, it was always you.
label: Misery

text:
there's that feeling
of old memories
and happy voices --
big smiles too,
whenever you hear
thag one tune.

that one simple melody.

that one chord.

it all floods back.
label: Nostalgia

text:
Do you ever tell lies? 
To yourself?
A lie,
The broken set of words 
Are either used to console 
Or destroy..
And if you are at the later end ...
It will hurt like hell
No matter how hard u try ..
TO fix things ...
And that is when u realise 
Broken pieces of a vase 
Can't be fixed ..
but still they look as beautiful 
As they were .....
Just simple , beautiful , And broken 
Not easy to mend 
But still 
Crucially important 
And it's not where it ends ..
U think ,think ,think and go on thinking 
Till your head hurts 
It begs u to stop 
And that's when you lie 
Lie to yourself 
"I WILL!"
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
Beneath the winter's frozen ground,
A seed holds summer in its heart.
Though silence reigns without a sound,
Life waits to play its part.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
The quiet joy of finding a good pen,
And then immediately losing it again.
It wrote so smoothly, and the ink was black,
A perfect tool, there is no coming back
From such a loss. All other pens will feel
Like clumsy fakes, a cheap and shabby deal.
I search my desk, a frantic, hopeless plea,
For the one pen that understood me.
label: Everyday Life

text:
The morning alarm, a shrill and angry beep,
A promise that the waking world must keep.
Five minutes more, a bargain that I make,
For just a sliver of a dream to take.
The day is waiting, patient and so vast,
A future built upon a sleeping past.
label: Everyday Life

text:
We are all stories, in the end, it seems,
A collection of our memories and dreams.
We tell ourselves the tale of who we are,
A guiding and familiar, northern star.
We edit out the parts we can't abide,
The moments where we failed, or ran, or lied.
And what is left is not the perfect truth,
But the essential, necessary proof
That we survived. The story is the thing
That gives our weary, broken spirit wing.
We are the author, and the hero too,
And the ghost-writer, seeing our own self through.
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
nostalgia feels like a rotting tooth

and it won't come out; no matter how many times i
wiggle and twist
and pull at it

or when my father tells me he's
going to tie a string around it and
slam the door

or my mother threatens to send me to the dentist
its too big of a problem for an ordinary person

im attached to the rot

she is my friend; i watched her grow
and she grew with me too
plus, ive never liked leaving things behind

and i remember-
how? can i remember her if she is nothing
how? will i be able to understand the present and survive the future
without the context of the past

the rot will spread and I will endure it

even so, it hurts
label: Nostalgia

text:
Who in this world could claim the right
To define what is a memory?

To be able to see what others can’t see,
To be able to smell flowers in dreams—
We are all a walking treasury.

What magic we make that grows with age
And creeps through our melodies,

That trickles from books, from lasting looks, from yawning gentle poetry.

What words can change in an hour or an age
Of long past tales and history?

Can we remember or try to dismember
The meaning of a eulogy?

Do we surrender to cold December
And live again in memories,
Or wish that someday we break asunder
And become immortal memories?
label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
The plane’s contrail, a chalk-line in the blue,
That splits the perfect sky in perfect two.
It starts as sharp, a brilliant, hopeful white,
And then it slowly blurs and fades from sight.
It is a scar that heals before your eyes,
A temporary wound that never dies,
But just dissolves. A reminder of a place
We cannot go, at an ungodly pace.
label: Everyday Life
Romantic

text:
There is a stranger who lives in my home,
He wears my clothes and uses my own comb.
He speaks with my voice, a venomous twin,
And praises all the trouble he gets in.
He is the architect of my own pain,
The one who stands and dances in the rain
While I am drowning. I would cast him out,
But he is me, of that there is no doubt.
label: Misery

text:
The laundry basket, a familiar weight,
A simple chore to contemplate my fate.
Folding the corners, smoothing out the seams,
Lost in the quiet of my waking dreams.
Another t-shirt, warm from the machine,
A small, repeated, ordinary scene.
label: Everyday Life

text:
I am a ship that’s wrecked upon the shore,
And cannot hear the ocean anymore.
The waves that brought me here have now withdrawn,
And left me broken, waiting for a dawn
That shows the damage, splintered wood and torn,
A lonely, ruined thing, and so forlorn.
I was built for the deep, the storm, the spray,
Not to be left to rot and waste away.
label: Misery

text:
What once crawled slow upon the ground
Now dances on the breeze.
Transformation can be found
In patient mysteries.
label: Hope&Joy

text:
You know I love it 
When I look out of my window
On a winter afternoon 
The lights peeking through the dusty rooms
Illuminating the trees with a golden glow
It so very much matches to you

You know I feel warm
When I take long walks 
Alone with my songs 
On sweet evenings 
dimming into a stary-lit sky
It kind of feels like your touch 

You know I feel Full
When am sitting in a cafe 
With my half eaten pizza and my book
Stealing glances at the street 
It's the most beautiful painting 
I have ever seen
It reminds me of your smile

You know I feel free
When m cycling through the valleys
Traveling alone with skies , roads and trees
The breeze brushes my skin 
Gently touching and soflty leaving 
It's the most shivering freedom
I have ever lived with
It associates itself to you

It's funny sometimes
How much I love it 
It's funny sometimes 
How much I crave it 
It's funny sometimes
How much I feel it
It's almost funny
But 
 your humour doesn't match with me
label: Misery

text:
Brian, I’m still calling you Brian,
it’s raining and Sunday where you gig
now, or it isn’t. I can only reconstruct

you from the stickers, scabbed
and bleaching on the punished body
of your gnarly Tanglewood.

The nurse reattached your fingertip,
but a wallop from the yard brush
ruptured your eardrum.

One less ear for tinnitus to fuck,
you’d smirk, no polished savant you
but your hands were charmed.

You’d oblige when a haze of stragglers
at the party passed the toy ukulele,
pluck until the big fella wept.

Ais, moonlighting as a dilettante
astrologer for pub money, saw collisions
in your nativity, tapping an ochre nail

on your tenth house of fame
in the horoscope she’d drawn in air
and that cinched it.

One time you said we’d tour Egypt.
But Brian I’ve pawned my tinny amp
and the Stratocaster wasn’t mine to keep.

Story with rent? Even a frontman
of your cut and sacrée tignasse
might be turfed out on a landlord’s whim.

But I know the story. Or I do its gist.
The scraggly contours. Box bedroom. Skint.
Don’t ask me how, but I feel

some tomorrow your name will occur
and I’ll shrug no news since this.
I’ll wish I asked then, why Egypt?

Maybe don’t wade out so far alone
some beautiful day in June
until the sound stops.

Just square this as a crappy phase
of the moon’s twenty-and-eight.
I want tortoiseshell plectrums for you.

Whisht is what you say, a querying thumb
jabbed in the direction of the bar
where the tune is jangling down.

You know what it is.
You’ve heard it before, you think—where?
It’s not my place to say.
label: Nostalgia

text:
I smile a lot these days 
Tend to notice and acknowledge 
The rustling of leaves 
And the dew-bathed ways
Was it always this beautiful?
Or was I just being ignorant ?
ofcourse I silently cherished the skies
The colors and the thunders
The lightning and the blunders 
But nowadays ,
I talk a little more 
Maybe way too much 
Abt this and that 
Well, It's nothing special as such 
The stories i carve 
Tangled threads need not to be solved 
As chaotic as the weather 
Sometimes a little sombre 
And a dangerous jubilation all together 
I like how it is 
Maybe its just you 
That makes me go all through 
Honestly, I don't mind 
Just stay 
Okay darling?
label: Romantic

text:
She walks in beauty, like the night
Of cloudless climes and starry skies;
And all that’s best of dark and bright
Meet in her aspect and her eyes;
Thus mellowed to that tender light
Which heaven to gaudy day denies.

One shade the more, one ray the less,
Had half impaired the nameless grace
Which waves in every raven tress,
Or softly lightens o’er her face;
Where thoughts serenely sweet express,
How pure, how dear their dwelling-place.

And on that cheek, and o’er that brow,
So soft, so calm, yet eloquent,
The smiles that win, the tints that glow,
But tell of days in goodness spent,
A mind at peace with all below,
A heart whose love is innocent!
label: Romantic

text:
A sunbeam cuts across the dusty room,
A golden blade that banishes the gloom.
And in its light, a secret world appears,
The floating dust of all the fallen years.
A million tiny specks, they swirl and gleam,
A silent, slow, and effervescent stream.
It is a galaxy in miniature,
A brief and accidental overture.
You cannot touch it, and you cannot hold,
A story made of particles of old.
It only lasts as long as the sun's there,
A fleeting universe made of thin air.
label: Everyday Life

text:

label: Philosophical & Reflective

text:
Summer mornings, as I round out
the bend in the well-traversed road,
the new sun imposes itself
in the cloudy rearview mirror,
and, for a moment, I am blind,
the way ahead obscured by gnats
of light teasing my weary eyes
the yellow white of the desk lamp
I set atop the chest of drawers
and tilted at such an angle
that I glowed as I emoted
to a rapt crowd of stuffed creatures
enthralling tales penned in crayon
on snap fresh construction paper,
words I knew would only better
as I much too slowly matured
into my God-given talents,
for my life would not be wasted
swatting the blinding memories
every blasted summer morning
as I crawl across the bridge
to the work side of the river
label: Nostalgia

text:
The house stood empty, black with char,
The fire took everything they owned,
The photos, books, the old guitar,
The rooms where love and life had grown.
They stood together in the yard,
Father, mother, children three,
The youngest asked, "This is too hard,
Where will we go? Where will we be?"
The mother knelt down in the ash,
She held her daughter's tear-stained face,
She said, "We are not burned to trash,
We still have love, we still have grace."
The father called his brother near,
The brother brought his truck and tools,
The neighbors came throughout the year,
Everybody breaking rules.
Of what one person could provide,
Together building something new,
Working side by loving side,
They raised the walls and roof anew.
The women brought the family meals,
The men all gave their Saturday,
The children played with squeals and squeals,
And made the reconstruction play.
And though the house would not be same,
Without the photos on the wall,
Without Great-grandma's picture frame,
Without the marks that showed them tall,
There was something beautiful born,
From tragedy and pain and loss,
A community that hadn't torn,
But gathered 'round to bear the cross.
The new house rose from ashes old,
With better beams and stronger floor,
With stories shared and tales retold,
And love embedded in each door.
They moved in on a autumn day,
When leaves were gold and crimson red,
And everybody came to stay,
And celebrate the life ahead.
This is hope's most powerful art—
Taking ruins and making home,
Finding courage in the heart,
To build again and not to roam.
This is joy beyond the surface bright,
The deep contentment that we find,
When community holds us tight,
And reminds us we're not left behind.
label: Hope&Joy
